title: Walmart kept my groceries
body: March 17th, I had eye surgery and went back on the 18th for a follow-up exam. After my appointment, I stopped at store #05080 1743 Precinct Lone Rd. to get some groceries with my fiancé.'  I paid for my groceries first and placed them on the counter while my fiancé completed his purchases. When we got to his car, I realized we did not get all my bags in the cart. We returned to the counter where we had checked out, and my bags were not there. We went to customer service to inquire what happened to the bags. We waited a very long time for information about my groceries, with no place for me to sit. My fiancé did some business with his bank located in the store and asked if I could use their chairs. Later, a Walmart manager says this is suspicious behaviour.  We have contacted the store and have never been able to speak to the manager. They see my bags on their feed and say a Walmart driver took them. and they do not intend to make it right. After many calls to the store and Walmart.com, I believe they have no intention of making this right.The accusations of our character when I was recovering from surgery and made an innocent mistake are unacceptable to me. Kroger and Target have better quality food anyway.Nancy Markoff

title: Walmart Class Action lawsuit on the Horizon
body: Walmart, like many large corporations, appears to track the political and regulatory climate closely—and capitalize when oversight is weak. Prioritizing short-term, quarterly shareholder gains over sustainable, stakeholder-centered practices creates long-term operational weaknesses, even if it looks efficient on paper.My recent experience highlights this problem clearly. I placed a delivery order for a 20-quart pot after my freezer failed—I needed to cook a large amount of seafood immediately. The order was confirmed, but when the driver arrived, a substitution was required. The issue? Walmart’s system does not allow meaningful upward substitutions. Anything smaller than 20 quarts was useless in my situation, making the delivery ineffective from the start.At the same time, I ordered a $2 pack of pre-cut celery. I live about 45 minutes from the Bonham location, and given Walmart’s scale, inventory systems, and seasonal rotation practices, it’s difficult to understand how multiple comparable options weren’t available—especially for a basic item like a stock pot.What made the situation worse was a breakdown in Walmart’s communication system. The driver attempted to message me through the app, but I never received it. I increased the tip to $20 to account for the inconvenience—he never saw that either. I tried calling, but the call wouldn’t go through, and I didn’t receive his message. Communication failed on both ends.Because of that failure, what should have been a simple coordination issue turned into a real expense. The driver lost time and fuel, and I ended up covering costs that could have easily been avoided. What started as a $2 item turned into a $42 outcome when factoring in delivery inefficiencies and return logistics.This isn’t just about one failed order. It reflects a broader operational model where friction, inefficiency, and risk are pushed downward onto both customers and frontline workers—while the company protects its margins and optimizes for short-term returns. Drivers absorb wasted time and fuel, customers absorb failed logistics, and the system continues operating as if those costs don’t exist.That kind of model may scale—but it doesn’t sustain trust.If Walmart wants to maintain long-term credibility, it needs to rethink how its substitution policies, communication systems, and delivery logistics impact real people—not just internal metrics or quarterly performance.If these systemic issues continue to go unaddressed, legal action—including a potential class action—will become an inevitable next step as accountability mechanisms catch up to corporate behavior.

body: I ordered an item to be delivered to my home. When I received notification that it had been delivered, I checked and found it in fact had not arrived. The photo that accompanied the notification showed the item sitting on a porch that was not mine. I called and reported this error on their part and was told I had to wait 3 hours and call back. I waited and called back as instructed and reported the error again and inquired when I could expect my item. I was told then to wait 48 more hours and call back. This is unacceptable.... I paid good money to get this item and due to their incompetence I do not have it as promised and am expected to wait 2 more days while the item they misdelivered is in an unknown location with absolutely no guarantee at this time that I will receive it. Furthermore, after viewing their website regarding the matter of not receiving items, they actually state that their carriers may mark an item as delivered BEFORE it actually is. Which leads to the logical conclusion that the falsifying of records may be commonplace in my opinion

As soon as the item is dropped off at UPS, Amazon issues your refund.  For all of the effort that Walmart goes through to compete with Amazon, they fall drastically short.  Customer Service was equally atrocious.  They kept ending the chat and the phone customer service was exceedingly rude.  Do yourself a favor and avoid walmart.com.

body: Very disappointed with Walmart’s service. I returned an item that was received at their warehouse on March 19, and as of today, I still haven’t received my refund. This delay is unacceptable.On top of that, this is not the first issue. When you place an order, they often send the wrong items, which creates even more inconvenience. Then you have to go through the return process, wait for pickup, and deal with long delays to get your money back.Customer service has been no help. They keep saying the issue will be resolved, but nothing actually happens. No follow-through, no urgency, and no real solution.Overall, very frustrating experience. title: 12-Hour Logistics Failure followed by Support Gaslighting
body: Total service failure from Walmart.com. Ordered a large set of mirrors at 9:30 AM for same-day delivery. After waiting 12 hours, support lied multiple times. First, saying a driver had the items, then admitting no driver was assigned. Despite the 15-minute easy interstate drive to my residence, they failed to fulfill the order and then suggested I pick it up myself in a sedan. To top it off, after canceling due to their own incompetence, they are holding $149.48 of my money for 'up to 10 days.' Absolute humiliation for any customer who expects basic competency. Here's another bit from their email "You won't be charged for these items. Any temporary hold placed on your payment method should be removed within 10 business days." I mean do what? HUH? They do realize that's contradictory right? Total service failure but then say I won't be charged for the items but they are going to hold my money for the next 10 days? If that's not contradictory and ignorant then I don't know what is. I don't know who writes this stuff but they really need to get their heads examined. Additionally, I'm not giving them a 0% interest loan for 10 days for their incompetence. I want my money. Not only did they gaslight me, and now holding my money like I loaned it to them, not delivering what I ordered, wasting an entire 12 hours of my life that I'll never get back. To top it off here's another kicker you know what I was offered for "my troubles" $10 off gift card of a purchase of $30 or more. I mean are they kidding me? I mean what? Are they insane? First of all because of the obvious lol umm no! Second, I would never order anything from them ever again. Nor will I shop in their stores. I have no desire to be gaslighted, condescended and treated like my time wasted for an entire day didn't matter.

I am disgusted that a multi-billion dollar company cannot handle something as simple as... taking an ID and physically going through the order with me when I arrive, because it is already put together under my name! I even called and mentioned my disability AND my low-income AND how I have to pay for someone to CONTINUALLY GO TO THE STORE to be met with "we don't know.." when to pick up the order"...come back later or keep calling and checking online." THIS IS RIDICULOUS! WALMART...I think we ALL, who have had to deal with this issue TODAY, should get a gas card reimbursement, AND/OR a Walmart gift card for the cost of this day! We did not even get an apology. Absolutely disgusting.

title: If I could rate Walmart.com with 0…
body: If I could rate Walmart.com with 0 Stars, I would. One star is extremely generous. I have been communicating for over a month with their live chat line, as well as speaking with several customer service representatives who simply provide empathetic lip service. When I asked to speak with a Customer Service Supervisor, I was waiting on hold for 45 minutes before he was able to speak with me and provide basically the same empathetic lip service. The issue is an online order that was flagged as delivered. As soon as the delivery email arrived I checked at the door but there was no parcel there. I went to the mail box to see if perhaps it was there. Of course it wasn't. I then walked around the community (over 200 townhouses) to see if maybe it was mistakenly delivered at a neighbor's house. Of course it wasnt. I rechecked the delivery email and found the delivery location image was TOTALLY BLACKED OUT.  That was of no assistance in locating the so-called delivered parcel. I contacted Walmart.com immediately and the lip service began. I have wasted likely over 7 hours of my time trying to get my money refunded or to have them resend the parcel, to no avail. I have asked them to contact the courier to determine what has happened to my parcel. They have indicated it would be investigated, but they refused to specify what they did to "investigate" the missing parcel. Apparently that investigation was through their "highest authority" and they refused to refund my $55.94.  I have had to go through my financial institution to launch a claim dispute against Walmart.com.The Walmart customer service rep indicated that was the only way I could possibly recover my money.  OK so Walmart got my money, likely the courier enjoyed the contents of my parce, and I was out $55.94.  This is how Walmart.com treats their customers. I know for a fact that I WILL NEVER ORDER ANOTHER ITEM FROM WALMART.COM  and I strongly suggest other customers think twice before placing an online order with them. You might get your order, you might not. If you dont get your order, good luck attempting to get your hard earned money refunded.

I will never buy another thing fro Walmart never.

title: Order never delivered, Walmart won’t solve the problem
body: I placed an online order that was never delivered. I reported the problem right away. Based on a note from the shipper, they delivered to the wrong address (there is a very similar address to mine in a neighboring town, which requires an access code for deliveries - no access code is required to deliver to my house). After three or four attempts to get Walmart to rectify the problem, including a complaint to Better Business Bureau, Walmart said that a refund would be a violation of their return policy. Instead, they suggested I contact my financial institution for a remedy and file a complaint with the shipper. This is a befuddling approach to customer (non)service, especially when there are so many other competing online retailers who have figured out to win repeat customers.

title: Walmart has many good qualities, but needs to improve in other areas.
body: Likes 👍🏻 1) They offer a great selection of products.2) Their pricing by in large is competitive with other companies.3) Their delivery times for shipped products are fairly quick a good part of the time.4) Their customer service on chat is generally friendly and helpful.5) On some occasions, they allow refunds without needing to return an unwanted or damaged product.6) For returning a product you've purchased, they offer returns via regular mail, return to the local Walmart and free FedEx pickup right on your porch.7) They offer an email communication option.Dislikes 👎 1) They don't allow customers to edit or delete reviews of purchases. Once you post a review for any purchases you make, the reviews are on the Walmart website permanently, This makes no sense whatsoever! Needs to be changed.2) Their membership is too expensive. It needs to be reduced by 50%, in my opinion.

title: Well, but it still has room for improvement
body: As a frequent Walmart customer, I’ve had the opportunity to visit many stores in different states. I’ve had both good and not-so-good experiences.  For example, at a Walmart in West Palm Beach, where I lost almost an hour trying to buy a container of breast milk because it was locked up and no one showed up to help.What I do like is the delivery option. For a reasonable monthly fee, I can plan my shopping and have everything delivered to my home, which is a huge plus. What I don’t love is finding out at the last minute that some items are out of stock, I think the app could do a better job giving faster and more accurate availability updates.  I like how the app shows my purchase history, which helps me remember items I buy frequently and check if I need them again.The extra perks, like streaming access and cash back rewards, are also a nice bonus. Overall, Walmart is convenient, but there are definitely a few things they could improve.

title: Love it
body: I’ve actually been using this service since they started near the end of the pandemic and I have had a consistently good experience using Walmart online grocery shopping. The website and app are easy to navigate, making it simple to find what I need and place orders quickly. I really appreciate the wide selection, affordable prices, and frequent substitutions that are clearly communicated ahead of time.Pickup and delivery have both been reliable, and my groceries are almost always accurate and well-packed. Fresh items like produce and meats are handled with care, which is something I value a lot when ordering online. On the rare occasion there’s an issue, customer service has been responsive and helpful.Overall, Walmart’s online grocery service has saved me a lot of time and stress, and it’s incredibly convenient for busy schedules. I highly recommend it to anyone looking for dependable, affordable online grocery shopping.

title: Usually great, but has issues.
body: I do a lot of my shopping at Walmart because of their prices. One thing I will say is, I HATE their mobile app! Like Amazon, it is filled with third-party sellers, which isn’t necessarily a bad thing, but sometimes makes it more difficult to find the items I need for pickup rather than shipping. I do a lot of pickup orders since I don’t usually have time to go in the store to shop, and the app makes it incredibly difficult! A lot of the time, an item I need won’t even show up on the app, but if I go in stores, I can find it. It’s really annoying and makes it harder to place orders, especially grocery orders, for pickup. They really need to fix their app. Another downside is their produce section. It isn’t bad, but you can definitely get better quality produce at other shopping locations, though they’re usually more expensive. Overall, Walmart is great, but it definitely has its downsides.

title: Lose AI and use customer service…
body: Lose AI and use customer service employees that don’t lie. Also when Walmart makes a mess out of an order, fix it NOW, have missing items pulled and delivered immediately. Don’t offer a refund for missing items that you won’t get for 8-10 business days!The order I placed this morning was incomplete. Half of my items were not delivered. I phoned the store it came from, after three tries to get someone to answer the phone, I was told I’d have to call Walmart.com. I’m telling them the order was delivered not even five minutes ago and delivery driver can come back. They told me it wasn’t possible and again, I’d have to call Walmart.com. What? The rep I spoke with told me they’d issue a refund that would take 8-10 business days and I’d have to re-order. What???? For Walmarts mistake??? After going around and around with this guy, he puts me on hold for ten minutes, comes back on and tells me he has spoken with his manager and the Account Review Team and they will take care of getting my order to me ASAP. I told him I need it today, not ASAP. He assured me several times after he said ASAP and I said today, that yes in fact it will be today. He tells me that he himself has sent an email stating in all caps EMERGENCY MUST HAVE TODAY! He also states his manager has done the same and have been sent to this review team. He tells me he will send me a folllw up email. Well the email indicates only one item was changed. So I call again and speak with a woman that keeps talking over the top of me and not paying attention to what I’m saying. She tells me I will not be receiving the missing items at all and to expect a refund in 8-10 business days. Wait a minute…the first guy assured me they had made and exception in this situation and I would receive my items. She ends up ending the call because she “couldn’t hear me”. I call back and immediately let this next gal I’d like a manager. She kept trying to assist even though I kept telling her I needed a manager. She finally sent me to a “supervisor” who told me the same…you won’t be receiving your missing items instead a refund in 8-10 business days. She could see a refund had been requested. I ask her…so that guy lied to me, or are you accusing me of telling the lie that arrangements were made for me to receive my items and no refund. Eventually I asked for a manager and was told the manager wouldn’t tell me anything different. Oh, and then the manager wasn’t available, but would call me back…still waiting…Bottom line Walmart messes up your order and do nothing but hold your money hostage for two weeks. The money I’m waiting for from them is my grocery money. I can’t re-order until I have my refund.

body: I recently ordered a few items from Walmart online and the process was straightforward. The prices were reasonable and the order arrived within the expected time frame. Everything matched the description, so overall I’m satisfied with the purchase.

title: Walmart declined the United Healthcare…
body: Walmart declined the United Healthcare card when my mother attempted to purchase wipes. We have previously ordered wipes online using the card, and these orders were fulfilled by Walmart. When we subsequently tried to purchase the same items in-store, the transaction was rejected. There is a remaining balance of twenty-three dollars on her card. Unfortunately, online ordering is not an option as it requires a minimum balance of thirty-five dollars, which is why we attempted the in-store purchase. The store associate was courteous but unable to resolve the issue. My mother is seventy-eight years old. It appears there is a discrepancy between the United Healthcare card application and Walmart's in-store system. It is perplexing that I can order the product online using the card for Walmart delivery, yet the same product is deemed ineligible for in-store purchase. This experience resulted in a significant waste of time and considerable frustration.I attempted to submit a review via the Walmart survey, ensuring all information was entered correctly and quadruple-checked, but the submission was rejected.

title: TOTALLY UNRELIABLE!
body: I am not a regular Walmart shopper but they seemed to have a good price on a small gas range with quick delivery so I took a chance. Big mistake. There was no updated delivery information other than to say on time. The range never showed up and I was not contacted by Walmart or their third-party vendor as to the reason for delay or when the next delivery date would be. Contacted Walmart and they were courteous enough but useless. In addition to this inexcusable lack of service, two previous small purchases were disasters as well. First, I received the wrong merchandise three times and the second, the quality of the item was so poor I'm shocked that anyone would think it was suitable to sell. Under no circumstances do I recommend Walmart and I will NEVER purchase anything from them again.

title: The woman who helped me at the drive up…
body: The woman who helped me at the drive up Walmart pharmacy was very helpful ! She informed me my insurance did not cover my prescription and checked Good RX , and it ended up at half the price ! She did this on her own and was so helpful that I think all my prescriptions will be at this pharmacy !

title: Walmart has turned for the worst
body: I do not understand how they can charge us to make an order just to pick up groceries OK and it's got to be over $35 and then they charge you extra $2 in case they have to be replaced items got to be replaced and last time they said my my item is supposed to be replaced I walked in there and every single thing that I they replaced was on the Dang shelf so I don't know what's really going on but they need to fix it

title: Horrific customer service
body: I recently purchased a sweater in a local Walmart and subsequently found it on sale on Walmart.com. As their policy is to not price match w/their online store, I returned it and contacted Walmart online support to buy it there. I asked to have the shipping waived for having been inconvenienced. Was told the $6.99 could not be removed at check out, but was promised a refund to my cc w/in 10 business days. I requested an email confirmation, and was told it could not be done, but it was all recorded on their end. A few days later I called again and was reassured that I would be refunded. Over 3 weeks passed and no refund. I called again and was told the previous 2 agents had not recorded anything, and had not been honest with/me regarding their policy of not refunding shipping . This agent promised he would now give me back my entire purchase price of $15.19 but I would again need to wait up to 10 business days to see it returned to my cc. He also explained I'd receive an email verification of the refund from Walmart w/in 48 hours.Well, w/in 2 hours I did receive a denial email which prompted me to make another call (as an aside, trying to get to a human is a nightmare as AI refuses to comply and will end the call... I was advised to say I want to speak w/a human" 3x and it worked). After an initial frustrating exchange w/ a rep. I asked for a supervisor who was extremely unhelpful. She said it couldn't be done even after she listened to the audio of all the false promises and tricks from the previous agents.She simply said those agents were wrong to promise me those things and they will be addressed. I will NEVER shop at Walmart again, either in store or most certainly not online.I made that purchase believing what I was promised, and would not have if I had known I was going to have that charge on my card. I am now seeking the assistance of my credit card company to have the charges reversed and am spreading the word so that others may avoid falling for this. It is clear from all the negative reviews that Walmart lacks basic understanding of customer service.

title: Their fraud prevention system goes too far
body: Their fraud prevention system is not working. I made an order and put the wrong address for my billing address by mistake. The order was promptly canceled. It also hangs up on you randomly and starts sentences that it doesn’t finish.I just went through hours of grief trying to order a product. I’m giving up and ordering it on Amazon.

title: Beware of Weasel Infestation!
body: Just because Walmart or a Walmart third party vendor shows an item being offered for sale does not mean the item is actually available. Some vendors will create a shipping label to falsely indicate that the item is now in the shipping process. Example: the purchase was made on the 23rd, this vendor created a shipping label on the 24th of February. After five days, I inquired if the item has been shipped yet. The vendor charged my account for the item on the 24th. Every inquiry I made I received a form letter stating that I should follow the tracking number. The tracking showed the item had not been received by the shipper yet. After 8 days with no progress on the tracking number, I asked the vendor to cancel the order. The vendor said the charge could not be canceled. However, the vendor managed to ship the item 13 days after the order was made. Clearly, the item was either in his possession when the request to cancel was made, or he was waiting for the order to come in since he never had the item in the first place. The shipping information on the Walmart site is not always accurate. Always go by the shippers tracking number. Unfortunately, the consumer must be vigilant for weasel infestations on corporate and third-party platforms.

Then Walmart thinks I should go looking and wait days for a refund. Yes I'm supposed to wait 3 to 5 days on frozen and refrigerated food. Would you tell me how to go upstairs in a wheelchair? Since I can't do that and haven't been able to talk reason to them a time or two they basically have stolen my money again.  I'm tired of them stealing and I've got to figure out something else.

title: I like Walter G and everything
body: I like Walter G and everything, but the best PR for Walmart is upset Amazon customers.We started using Amazon Prime while living in Ireland.  My job took my wife and I there for 4 year (2017-2021) so part of this period Ireland was in lock-down due to COVID.  We had gotten decent results with Amazon despite not having a fulfillment center on the island (Yes, for a large majority of voting age US citizens, Ireland is on an island (and it's own country)).  We became very dependent on Amazon at that point.Fast forward and we are back in the States and now it's 2026.We recently moved to an apartment/home complex in Uptown Dallas Texas.  The complex has 2 independent sides separated by the club house / office / pool / gym.  Each side has their own vehicle gate with a code.  We moved in in later December 2025 and started to order the kinds of things you do after a move when you realize you need something different or extra for a specific space.  We continued to utilize Amazon and Amazon used the 'common' are mail room as their dumping ground for daily deliveries.  They back up to the clubhouse, unload everything into a bin, roll it into the mail room and dump it in the middle of the room.  Then the office staff have to review every package, mark it with the unit number in big bold print and place it on a shelf or near if the item is too large.  Found the section in the Amazon My Account to update our address to include the gate code for our half of the complex.  Ensured everything was correctly logged and saved it.  Guess what happened, nothing.  Despite being provided the EXACT address and details our packages continued to be delivered to the mail room dumping ground for ALL of the delivery organizations (FedEx, UPS, Amazon, USPS, and etc.).  Now we spend (spent) A LOT on Amazon delivery - a LOT.  Did a quick round DOWN and we currently have $498 in PENDING.  For the date of 27 Feb alone, even subtracting the return credits, we spent $169.  Using conservative math of $170 per day at 4 days per month for 12 months, we spent (using this math equation) we spent over $8,160.00 in 12-months.  This doesn't take into account the huge amount we spent in the make ready and move in costs for this newest location.  I justify the costs by saying my wife doesn't like to drive (won't drive in Uptown Dallas TX), we only own 1 vehicle and therefore, i gas tank to feed and 1 vehicle to ensure.  She does take Uber when needed but simple math gives you, for a decent vehicle, monthly payments at $500 per month (add fuel, maintenance, insurance, valet car park, parking lot rash repairs (someone slams open their door into your car or hits your parked vehicle and drives away) and you've blown through the $8,160 YOY average out of pocket costs.Back to the Amazon trashing, despite having the full address and instructions, Amazon (or their affiliates, I know the drill) they continue to deliver our packages DOWN the street to the office.  Voiced our concern in the only way possible, the mindless CHAT session feature.Now that they have acknowledged what we have in our delivery instruction, the delivery individuals, state on the delivery notification that it was delivered to 'the front door or porch' and then provide the proof of delivery with a picture of the mail room, stuffed with packages.  Let that sink in.  They went out of their way to say it was delivered in accordance with our written instructions and then demonstrate that they lied, by taking a picture of the over stuffed mail room.  I really wish I could attached screen shots or photos to illustrate the issue.The office is located at a street address of 3711, our building is 3607 on the same street.  3711 does not equal 3607, even using the 'new' math (different rant different sight).  3711 and 3607 on the same street ARE NOT the same address.  Considering the street is one-way, we live 'down' the street from 3711 which means we would have to get in a car, drive around the block to approach 3711 from UP street to retrieve our packages.Anticipating this back and forth and upcoming spine surgery, every time it was delivered to out front door or porch AND had a picture of the mail room we advised it was a THUMBS DOWN delivery and checked either wrong address or failed to follow delivery instructions.  You'd think after 3 or 4 THUMBS DOWN deliveries that someone higher up the food chain would take notice for a customer who spends more that $8,160.00 per year (at a minimum).  Not a peep.  We are now into March 2026 and had 2 deliveries (it's only 02 March if you think it through)  done per our instructions - 2 whole deliveries!  Pat your self on the back Amazon...except there was an intervention on our end.  After having 6 packages delivered to our front door or porch (AKA the Mail Room for Amazon) with the photographic evidence showing the mail.. Running out of room - will pick up in another post.

title: I worked as a delivery driver for…
body: I worked as a delivery driver for Walmart for four years. During that time, I maintained a strong work record. One day, I made a mistake while driving and accidentally clipped the side of a wheelchair holder attached to the back of a truck that was sticking out farther than expected. I fully acknowledge that this was my error.After the incident, I began looking for a nearby parking space so I would not block traffic in the parking lot. While doing this, a cart attendant picked up a piece of my vehicle’s bumper that had fallen off. As I was parking, I heard him yelling at me to come back. I explained that I was only trying to park safely and out of the way.He then followed me to where I parked and approached me aggressively while holding the broken bumper piece. His behavior made me uncomfortable, as he was swinging the object around in a way that felt threatening. I calmly asked him multiple times to return my property, but he refused and became increasingly hostile.When I attempted to retrieve the bumper piece, he continued swinging it and ended up cutting me. At that point, I reacted by taking the item back from him to prevent further escalation or injury.Police were called to the scene, and afterward Walmart issued me a lifetime ban. I believe this outcome was unfair, considering I was assaulted and treated aggressively despite having provided delivery services for Walmart for many years.I believe Walmart, specifically Store #2075 in Bend, Oregon, handled this situation in an extremely unfair and unacceptable manner. The way this incident was addressed was deeply disappointing and does not reflect accountability or fair treatment.I acknowledge my own mistake in the initial accident; however, the events that followed should never have escalated the way they did. Instead of addressing the aggressive and threatening behavior displayed by a store employee toward me, Walmart chose to issue a lifetime ban against me — despite me being the person who experienced hostility and physical harm.I am asking Walmart to take responsibility for how this situation was handled. After providing delivery services for years, I expected professionalism, fairness, and a proper investigation into the employee’s conduct. Being permanently banned under these circumstances feels unjustified and disproportionate to what actually occurred.Because of this, I am now considering pursuing legal action to address the damages and unfair treatment resulting from this incident.

title: Live better save money Crook Mart inc
body: Live better save money Crook Mart inc. I was a customer for years online only. Then I stopped buying for 14'months, renewed my membership 11 days ago, came back recently. Now, Walmart has "subscription" authority over your bank debit card which means they can charge you anytime, anywhere they want even if the card is turned off. They charge me $144.00 for $95.00 worth of groceries delivered.nothing was returned to me ( refund or holds) customer service is useless. Another occasion I ordered a refrigerator $266.00, on my debit it actually says -$266.00 but my balance is the same! Same amount! No money was deducted!:3 days later $300.00 dollars come off my card. That's what the actual balance reads -$300.00,  yet when I scrolled down to The actual charge on my banking app it says -$266. So for a $266 frig. $300 comes off the card yet all I see is -266 in my truist app as I scroll down.On many orders they're taking money off the card 3DAYS later for an order,not right away like they use to.They're holds,charges , negatives,debits, credits, delayed billing,A WHOPPING 10 DAYS TO GET BACK HOLDS reads like a trigonometry math equation, it's just a way for them to confuse the customer and steal money. Let's face it when you have millions of customers and you steal just a dollar from each one,just one dollar. That is millions daily, free money and apps are the perfect perfect way to do this.I keep it simple, how much money did they take? How much groceries did they deliver? Then I compare the two and with just 11 days into my renewed membership 2 orders didn't add up they stole $90 total sofar with they're B.S accounting holds system, the money just disappears off the card. My advice to the consumer: when Crook Mart is "out of something" and or when they still charge you an enormous "Hold" please count your money. I have since cancelled my membership because it's too much aggravation and cost, not to mention being on the  phone with customer service each time I make an order. I'll still shop in store only, if I can't get it elsewhere first. This way I pay Crook Mart upfront and they give me groceries, for exact change . title: Walmart's "Guarantee" is as Broken as my 4-Month-Old Samsung TV.
body: The Review:Accusing a customer of damaging a stationary, wall-mounted or stand-mounted TV after merely four months is not an act of “troubleshooting”—it is a desperate maneuver to evade a warranty obligation.I purchased this Samsung QLED from Walmart less than 120 days ago, placing my trust in a reputable retailer to uphold the quality of the products they offer. Instead, I find myself ensnared in a corporate quagmire where Walmart and Samsung are engaged in a blame game, leaving me without a functional TV.The communication breakdown is astonishing. Since February 15th, I have been assured of callbacks that never materialize and solutions that remain elusive. Samsung has effectively “ghosted” me after casting aspersions on my integrity by alleging that I damaged the device, and Walmart has provided no assistance in addressing the issue with their vendor.I did not pay Walmart for a “malfunction and a middle finger.” I invested in a premium product that should endure for years, not mere weeks. Walmart’s “satisfaction guarantee” is evidently a mere illusion if they are willing to allow their loyal customers to be intimidated by a manufacturer’s inept service team.

title: Walmart introductory service Prices aren't REAL !!
body: Walmart introductory service Prices aren't Real !Full Disclosure:  (a) They completed an oil Change on my 2022 Ford F-150 in timely fashion, carefully, and professionally.   Excellent work !HOWEVER, (b) my wife later made an appointment for her Mercedes, so they knew almost 10 days in advance which brand and model of the Mercedes she drives and which type of oil would be required to complete that oil change service.  Unfortunately, when she arrived at Walmart, they told her that the type of oil needed for her car was NOT stocked in the Walmart garage, but that type WAS stocked out on the Auto Department Sales floor.  That seemed acceptable to her, so she OK'd the work.   But, when the work was completed, instead of the advertised $24.88 oil change price, WALMART charged her SIX [6] times that amount, simply because the oil needed was taken from the Sales Area instead of the from Garage Stock.    TOTAL RIP-OFF !!!.  My thought was if they advertise a service price, and then 'Bait-n-Switch' on their final pricing, they are essentially breaking the law.  Walmart has good employees, but when a customer makes an appointment almost TEN [10] days in advance, WALMART should have ZERO [0] excuse for making service price increases, since they had plenty of notice to check if they stocked the correct type of oil to complete that service appointment. EXTREMELY poor Customer Service.  WALMART, the largest retailer in the world, should be able to treat their customers much better. DOUBLE CHECK with those WALMART BOZO'S before making an oil-change appointment.   That zoo is managed by the animals.

title: Greeneville
body: Greeneville, TN Walmart Auto Center.  Brought my tire in to be repaired on 01-17-26, was informed that it was unrepairable and had a new tire put on in its place; I put the wheel with the new tire on it in my trunk.  On 01-28-26, I put the wheel with the new tire on my car to replace the spare.  On 01-30-26 I drove 75 miles to Knoxville and checked into a motel.  When I came out of the motel office to drive my car around to where my room was, this new tire was flat as a board.  Since the Walmart Auto Center in Alcoa was closed the next day due to the snowstorm, I brought the tire in to the Alcoa Walmart Auto Center on 02-01-26, was told this brand new tire that had no puncture marks or any other sign of road damage was unrepairable, and had another brand new tire put on to replace the original brand new tire.  The next day I left for a two-week vacation in Hawaii (I know, no sympathy now).  Upon returning to the airport, my second brand new tire was still inflated, and we drove home to Greeneville.  The next day, I brought the original brand-new tire to the Greeneville Walmart Auto Center where that tire had been purchased and installed onto the wheel of my car and asked for a refund since the tire had gone flat and become unrepairable for no apparent reason.  Both the assistant manager and the manager of the Greeneville Walmart Auto Center told me there would be no refund since I had not purchased the road hazard policy.  I pointed out and they verified that there had been no road hazard related damage to the tire, but they still refused a refund.  If I bought a pair of shoes at Walmart and the first time I wore them the sole fell off, I am sure Walmart would refund the purchase price or give me a new pair of shoes even though Walmart does not sell shoes with a warranty.  Why a tire would be any different, I don't know.

title: I cannot recommend Walmart
body: I cannot recommend Walmart, particularly the store located at 16313 New Independence Parkway, Winter Garden, FL 34787.Approximately one year ago, I purchased a bread machine from this location. After only three months of normal household use, a gear connected to the mixing bowl assembly failed. The internal mechanism became loose, causing ingredients to leak during operation and rendering the machine unusable.In 2025, I contacted Walmart to seek assistance. I was directed to contact the manufacturer. When I reached out to the manufacturer, they declined to help, stating that the product was not purchased through their official Amazon store and advised me to resolve the matter with Walmart instead. I then returned to Walmart, only to be redirected back to the manufacturer once again.This back-and-forth left me without any meaningful support. Now, one year after the original purchase and nine months with a defective product, I was informed that no action could be taken. I was even advised to file a dispute with my bank, which is no longer possible due to the time elapsed.As a customer, I expected accountability and reasonable support for a product that failed shortly after purchase. Instead, I experienced a cycle of deflection between retailer and manufacturer, with no resolution offered.Very disappointing experience and lack of customer support.

title: Someone chewed on my Walmart Account
body: This is a new one = so I had all my info on Walmart for about six months = where they send you a password and you then can buy what you ordered but last Friday night I was doing something and notice I had been charged by walmart to the tune of almost 5000 dollars = surprise well my bank had already put a stop on this = I don't even keep over a 1000 in the slush fund any given time = the charge was for three apple computers and it was addressed to a city an hour away with a street address - looking that up it is not in a good part of that city but it was a rental house = anyway I canceled my account right away - they kept wanting me to check back in = nope - seemed to me that would be away for them to get directly into my account = the last four on my card were wrong that they had used = even from the past = so the next morning I went to bank and they checked everything and nothing had happened and they had no reason why someone would do this = except as I said to piggy back into my account when they were asking me a password to get into the existing account. So if any of you read this be aware = I never feel good about leaving my stuff up = even on ebay I clear it after buying = seems like the tech to steal from you is just getting better and better and the banks will not be able to keep up with the charge backs. I don't have to tell any of you how sickening it is to realize how easy it is getting for others to rip you off. but if you have a walmart account I would be watching it = I guess my ordering from walmart is over = and I am old and now I will have to just go myself - and some stuff you want you can't get local and that blows = know what I mean Jelly Bean? ==
